compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Concrete evidence from the PERSISTED completion sidecars (COMPLETION_VERDICT events; NO re-run / no new LLM calls):

ad9f-45f1-339f-4346 — two verifier runs minutes apart, IDENTICAL code at HEAD:
- Run A (COMPLETION_VERDICT @ 1784840651): verdict FAIL, 2 unmet:
    (1) '...verify-commit-ticket ... auto-mounts ... — regression test'
    (2) 'explain (and init/scratch) are NOT force-mounted — guard test'
  Both details EXPLICITLY acknowledge the implementation exists (cited _cli/__init__.py:406-407, _init.py ensure_store_mounted_best_effort, _NO_AUTO_MOUNT line 42) — the criterion was marked unmet only because the verifier did not confirm/credit a TEST.
- Run B (COMPLETION_VERDICT @ 1784840960): verdict PASS, 0 unmet — the SAME two criteria => True.

Both tests DO exist (tests/interfaces/facades/test_central_mount_gate_ad9f.py: a fresh-clone verify-commit-ticket regression test AND an explain no-force-mount guard test). So the non-determinism is NARROWLY in the verifier's TEST-EXISTENCE crediting on criteria phrased '... — regression test / guard test': Run A false-negatived both; Run B credited both. The implementation assessment was STABLE across runs (recognized both times); only the test acknowledgment flipped.

Refined hypothesis: the flip is concentrated on 'is there a test for this?' judgments, where the verifier must locate test coverage in the tree. Sampling variance flips borderline 'couldn't find it' vs 'found it'. Suggested mitigations (unchanged) especially benefit here: (a) deterministic decoding; (b) for '... test' criteria, a deterministic pre-pass that greps/enumerates test files and feeds the verifier the concrete candidates so 'did a test exist' is not left to free-form search; (c) multi-sample quorum.

Contrast (NOT flakiness): 4de6 COMPLETION_VERDICT @ 1784841301 FAIL/1-unmet was a TRUE positive — it correctly cited the no-op 'Re-close to certify against the current tree' message at transition_close.py:688-692. Fixed in change 937. This shows the verifier's true-positives are reliable; the instability is specifically false-NEGATIVES on test-existence.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
CORRECTION / refinement (looked at the STORED cause; did NOT re-run — re-running only adds a sample, not a cause, and assumes a replicability we can't justify from the record):

1) Stored cause (ad9f FAIL COMPLETION_VERDICT @ 1784840651): both unmet findings' own `detail` acknowledge the IMPLEMENTATION exists (cite _cli/__init__.py:406-407, _init.py ensure_store_mounted_best_effort, _NO_AUTO_MOUNT line 42) and mark the criterion unmet ONLY for lack of a confirmed TEST. PASS @ 1784840960 credited the same two. So the verifier's own recorded reasoning localizes the flip to TEST-EXISTENCE crediting, not code assessment.

2) SIDECAR DIAGNOSABILITY GAP (new, concrete): diffing the two full records — same model (anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6), same runner (pydantic_ai), material_fingerprint=None in BOTH, coverage={} in BOTH. The COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecar does NOT persist the verified head_sha / verified_at_sha, nor a populated coverage, nor the input context. Therefore the record ALONE cannot prove the two runs had identical input — so 'it is a replicable flake' is not something the sidecar justifies. This is itself a defect: the sidecar is too slim to diagnose the verifier's own non-determinism. Fix: record the verify-sha, the resolved input snapshot id, model settings (temp/seed), and per-criterion evidence in the sidecar (this is the 'record WHY structurally' item, now with a concrete missing-field list).

3) Input-identity here is established by TIMELINE, not the record: both were ad9f closes from the same worktree at origin/main ~5min apart with no commits/HEAD movement between (one reset --hard origin/main, nothing else), and the 4de6 fix pins HEAD at entry, so both closes pinned the same commit. Identical input + flipped verdict => genuine non-determinism on test-existence.

Net: the mitigations still hold, and (2) is a prerequisite — until the sidecar records the verify-sha + inputs + decoding settings, non-determinism cannot be distinguished from input drift purely from stored data.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
LIVE RECURRENCE (strongest evidence yet): closing 4de6 just now, the completion verifier FAILED with a criterion marked unmet because 'No such test exists anywhere in the test suite ... exhaustive searches for TOCTOU, drift, close-ticket, HEAD-move, completion-race, and 4de6 in tests/ all returned no matches' — for the criterion 'a test that moves HEAD in the verify→sign window'.

That test PLAINLY EXISTS and is collectible on origin/main:
  tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_close_default_pins_head_4de6.py::test_default_close_pins_head_across_a_concurrent_commit
  (committed in 55e2b5cca, the 4de6 carrier; `pytest --co` collects 2 tests; the file name literally contains '4de6' and the test moves HEAD mid-verify via a racing verify stub and asserts the close signs against the pre-move sha).

So the verifier's 'exhaustive search for 4de6' missed a file NAMED *_4de6.py containing the ticket id. This is the same TEST-EXISTENCE false-negative characterized above (ad9f), reproduced on a different ticket. It also confirms the impact direction that matters most: the verifier BLOCKS complete work (false FAIL), and the block is non-deterministic (ad9f fl\ipped FAIL→PASS on retry). Directly motivates BOTH fixes scoped here: temperature 0 (reduce the sampling variance that flips these searches) + recorded verify_at_sha (so a false-FAIL can be checked against the actual verified tree from stored data).

compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem
Discovered while closing tickets this session. The completion-verification gate's LLM verifier (rebar verify-completion / the close-gate _completion_precheck) returns NON-DETERMINISTIC verdicts for IDENTICAL inputs — same ticket, same code at HEAD, same acceptance criteria, same ticket comments — within minutes and with no changes between runs.

## Evidence (ticket ad9f-45f1-339f-4346, code at origin/main HEAD, unchanged between runs)
- `rebar verify-completion ad9f --output json` standalone -> verdict PASS, 0 findings.
- `rebar transition ad9f in_progress closed` (same gate, moments later) -> "completion verification FAILED — 2 unmet criteria; not closing".
- Immediate retry `rebar transition ad9f in_progress closed` -> PASS, ticket closed.
Three runs, three effectively different outcomes (PASS / FAIL-2 / PASS) with no intervening change.

## Impact
- The close gate is unreliable in BOTH directions: it can spuriously BLOCK a legitimately-complete ticket (wasting an LLM run and forcing retries) and — the more dangerous direction — spuriously PASS a genuinely-incomplete ticket on a lucky run. A gate whose verdict flips on identical input cannot be trusted to certify completion.
- Interacts badly with the completion close mechanics (see 4de6): flaky FAILs during a close are expensive (billed LLM) and confusing; flaky PASSes undermine the whole attestation.

## Root-cause hypothesis
LLM sampling non-determinism: the verifier runs the model without pinned/deterministic decoding (temperature/seed) and/or without multi-sample aggregation, so per-run sampling variance flips borderline criteria between upheld/unmet. Model: pydantic_ai/claude-* per config.

## Suggested fixes
- Pin decoding for the verifier (temperature 0 and/or a fixed seed where the provider supports it) so identical inputs are reproducible.
- Multi-sample majority/quorum vote per criterion with a declared threshold (the plan-review gate already uses a multi-pass verify structure; mirror it).
- Distinguish a genuine FAIL from a flaky one: on a non-PASS close, allow a bounded retry/quorum before failing closed, and surface a 'low-confidence / non-reproducible' signal rather than a hard FAIL.
- Add a determinism regression check (run the verifier N times on a fixed fixture; require identical verdicts).

## Relation to our changes
UNRELATED to the 790c/80af/ad9f/4de6 code changes — this is the LLM-verifier layer (verify_completion), not the git-tree/ref plumbing those touched.

## Possibly related (same session / same subsystem)
- review-code silent-empty output when invoked non-interactively/backgrounded (exits 0 with an empty result — indistinguishable from a clean 0-findings pass). (From the 4de6 report.)
- PlanReviewGenerationChanged raised with IDENTICAL before==after generation hashes; a blind re-run signs. Also a currency/consistency check that fails against itself.

## Scope of THIS ticket (agreed) + Acceptance Criteria
Resolve e458 with two concrete, testable changes; the residual multi-sample quorum / deeper determinism hardening is a separate follow-up (see idea 4217 family).
- [ ] The COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecar payload (BOTH the PASS `completion_verifier_pass_v1` and FAIL `completion_verifier_fail_v1` schemas, via build_payload) records `verified_at_sha` (the immutable commit the verifier ran against; None in local mode) and `trace_id` (the model-run id), so two sidecars can be compared for input-identity from stored data alone — verified by a unit test on build_payload for both PASS and FAIL.
- [ ] The completion verifier pins model sampling temperature to 0 (greedy) by default — cfg.temperature is set to 0.0 in _verify_completion_inner UNLESS the operator explicitly set REBAR_LLM_TEMPERATURE (an explicit value wins), mirroring the model/step-floor tuning already there and the plan-review Pass-2 verifier's greedy pin — verified by a test that the tuned cfg.temperature is 0.0 by default and preserves an explicit non-None value.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
Note: temperature 0 is a MITIGATION (reduces sampling variance), not a proof of determinism; combined with the recorded verified_at_sha it makes any residual drift observable (identical input, identical decoding) rather than ambiguous.
compiled_state.title: completion verifier is non-deterministic: identical inputs yield PASS vs N-unmet-criteria across runs, making the close gate unreliable
